data.title: Hormel's anemic sales weigh on profitability despite price hikes
data.description:
Hormel’s (NYSE:HRL) decision to increase prices last year to offset inflationary pressure on commodity prices was reflected in anemic sales growth for the fiscal second quarter and a decay in profitability, contributing to a 2.5% drop in shares into Thursday’s open.

During the quarter that ended on April 27, Hormel (NYSE:HRL) realized a 0.3% increase in net sales to $2.9B, reflecting a 7% decline in volume and flat sales in both retail and foodservice, both of which were impacted by reduced commodity shipments and, in the case of foodservice, soft industry demand.

While profits were up in certain segments, the company’s overall profit was lower than a year ago to $0.35 per share, a penny better than expected, but 3 cents less than last year.

For FY25, Hormel (NYSE:HRL) now expects net sales to be between $12.0B to $12.2B from prior guidance of $11.9B to $12.2B, less than the $12.11B consensus estimate. Hormel (HRL) also raised the lower end of its organic net sales range to up 2% to up 3% from its prior outlook for 1% to 3% growth.

Adjusted earnings are now expected to be between $1.58 to $1.68 per share from earlier guidance of $1.58 to $1.72 per share, still bracketing the $1.60 estimate.

data.title: Trigon Metals to sell Kombat Project in Namibia to Horizon Corp for $24M in all-cash deal
data.description:
• Trigon Metals (TSXV:TM:CA) has signed a definitive agreement to sell its Kombat project in Namibia to Horizon Corporation and its affiliate Kamino Minerals Limited.
  • The all-cash transaction is valued at $24M.
  • Kamino will acquire 100% of Trigon’s shares in Trigon Ontario and PNT Financeco Corp., as well as the intercompany loan (PNT Loan).
  • The purchase consideration will be paid in eight equal instalments, beginning nine months after shareholder approval and Namibian competition clearance.
  • A pre-closing reorganization will transfer key liabilities and agreements, including the Sprott stream and project-related debt, into Trigon Ontario.
  • Additional cash payments to Trigon may be triggered by underground production milestones, ranging from US$3.5M to US$13M, and a copper royalty of 1% if copper prices exceed $4/lb.
  • Trigon is expected to receive $2M upfront following shareholder approval as part of the total consideration.
  • The Horizon Loan and Additional Loan are restructured in conjunction with the transaction; any project-related debt will be absorbed by the new owner.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Perimeter Medical Imaging AI announces $2.5M public offering
data.description:
• Perimeter Medical Imaging AI (TSXV:PINK:CA) announced the pricing and terms of a public offering of units and pre-funded units.
  • The offering is on a “reasonable best efforts” basis for a minimum of 8.33M securities at $0.30/unit or $0.29999/pre-funded unit, targeting gross proceeds of at least $2.5M.
  • Each unit includes one common share and one warrant, while each pre-funded unit includes one pre-funded warrant and one warrant.
  • Each warrant allows the purchase of one common share at $0.35, exercisable for five years from the closing date.
  • Proceeds will support commercialization, clinical validation, product development, and general corporate purposes.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: CAAP increases stake in Toscana Aeroporti
data.description:
• Corporación América Airports (NYSE:CAAP) said on Thursday that it has acquired, through a wholly-owned subsidiary, the remaining 25% interest in Corporación América Italia from the Investment Corporation of Dubai, thereby increasing its total interest in CAI to 100%. 
  • CAI holds a 62.28% interest in Toscana Aeroporti, the company’s subsidiary that operates the Pisa and Florence airports.
  • The transaction was completed through a share swap agreement, with CAAP delivering 1,996,439 newly issued shares to ICD as consideration for the 25% interest in CAI. 
  • With this acquisition, CAAP has increased its indirect economic interest in Toscana Aeroporti by 15.6%, further strengthening its strategic position in the Italian airport sector.
  • Corporación América Airports (NYSE:CAAP) shares were down 1.76% in pre-market trading.

data.title: Multi Ways Holdings regains compliance with NYSE continued listing standards
data.description:
• Multi Ways Holdings (NYSE:MWG) has received a notification from the NYSE Regulation that it officially regained compliance with exchange listing requirements.
  • On May 23, 2025, the Co. filed its 2024 Form 20-F, and was notified by NYSE Regulation on May 27, 2025, that it has regained compliance with its applicable NYSE American continued listing requirements.

data.title: TransDigm raises offer price for Servotronics to $47/share
data.description:
Servotronics (NYSE:SVT) +23.1% pre-market Thursday after saying TransDigm (NYSE:TDG) increased its tender offer price to acquire the company to $47/share in cash from $38.50/share in cash; TransDigm -3.4% pre-market.

Servotronics (NYSE:SVT) said the merger agreement was amended after it notified TransDigm (NYSE:TDG) that it had received an unsolicited acquisition proposal from a third party, however, its directors determined the third-party offer does not constitute a superior proposal.

Two weeks ago, the companies announced a merger agreement valued at ~$110M.

Servotronics (SVT) is a designer and manufacturer of servo controls and other advanced technology components for aerospace and defense applications, generating $45M in revenues for FY 2024.

data.title: Stock index futures gain as court strikes down Trump's April tariffs; Nvidia results bring cheer
data.description:
Stock index futures pared some gains on Thursday as the rally driven by a court striking down President Trump's April tariffs seemed to fade, but Nvidia's (NVDA) results and guidance continued to lift the mood.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.8%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +1.4%, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.3%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was flat at 4.48%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 1 basis point to 3.99%.

A federal trade court has struck down the “reciprocal” tariffs that Trump imposed on what he called “Liberation Day,” putting an immediate stop to the collection of a sweeping set of levies that the president imposed.

“We should say at the outset that this (the court ruling) is hardly the end of this story, as the administration are appealing this decision. But already, markets have seen a substantial rally in response,” Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

AI darling Nvidia (NVDA) rose 5.3% in premarket trade after the firm reported fiscal first-quarter results and guidance that topped expectations, despite the impact of added export control curbs to China.

“The tariff developments have overshadowed the previous day’s news, but sentiment also got a boost from Nvidia’s results after the U.S. close yesterday, as the company delivered upbeat sales guidance,” Allen added.

For now, the court ruling lifts a significant tax burden from US consumers and companies. However, the main outcome is uncertainty, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Looking towards the economic calendar now, there are a few macroeconomic data along with several Federal Reserve speakers lined up on Thursday.

U.S. Q1 GDP decline pared to -0.2% in second estimate.

Initial jobless claims jumped past consensus in the last week, while Q1 U.S. corporate profits dropped 3.6% Q/Q.

Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee and San Francisco Fed President Mary Daly, along with a few other Fed officials, will be speaking later in the day at different times and events. 

Wall Street ended lower Wednesday, with a noticeable downward move in the final hour of trading triggered by a report that the U.S. government had ordered chip designers to stop selling to China.

data.title: Immunic shares fall 12% after pricing of $65 million underwritten public offering to fund trials
data.description:
Immunic (NASDAQ:IMUX) on Thursday announced the pricing of an underwritten public offering consisting of (i) pre-funded warrants to purchase common stock, (ii) Series A warrants to purchase common stock, and (iii) Series B warrants to purchase common stock.

The combined public offering price of one pre-funded warrant, one Series A Warrant, and one Series B Warrant, which are being sold together but are immediately separable, is $0.7499. 

The initial gross proceeds from the offering, expected to close on June 3, are approximately $65 million. 

Shares of the biotechnology company slipped over 12% in premarket trading, and has lost nearly 9% in value YTD.

The company intends to use the net proceeds received from the proposed offering to fund its clinical trials and operations and for other general corporate purposes.

Immunic (NASDAQ:IMUX) may receive up to an aggregate of $130 million of additional gross proceeds if the Series A Warrants and Series B Warrants are exercised in full for cash.

data.title: Booz Allen declares $0.55 dividend
data.description:
• Booz Allen (NYSE:BAH) declares $0.55/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 2.1%
  • Payable June 27; for shareholders of record June 11; ex-div June 11.
  • See BAH Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: ARB IOT to conduct reverse stock split
data.description:
• ARB IOT (NASDAQ:ARBB) said the firm has approved a one-for-fifteen reverse share split of the company’s issued and unissued ordinary shares.
  • The split will begin on June 2.
  • The firm said the split is intended to increase the per-share trading price of the shares to help the company regain compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price rule.
